name: Clarifying wined3d, dxvk, opengl, and vulkan : r/linux_gaming - Reddit
snippet: DXVK is DX9-11. The wine project has its own DX12 to Vulkan translation layer. Wine has full DX9->Vulkan translation. WineD3D now has a Vulkan ...

name: GitHub - doitsujin/dxvk: Vulkan-based implementation of D3D8, 9
snippet: DXVK. A Vulkan-based translation layer for Direct3D 8/9/10/11 which allows running 3D applications on Linux using Wine. For the current status of the project ...

name: DXVK (DirectX-over-Vulkan) - stability improvements & potential
snippet: DXVK is a Vulkan-based graphics API translation layer for Direct3D 9/10/11 developed for running 3D applications on Linux using Wine.

name: Why doesn't Wine include DXVK by default? - WineHQ Forums
snippet: The main reason Wine doesn't include DXVK by default is because DXVK depends on Vulkan support, which isn't universally available or stable ...

name: DXVK - LinuxReviews
snippet: DXVK is a DirectX 9, 10 and 11 to Vulkan translation layer for Wine. It performs a lot better than Wine's built-in DX9-11 translayer and it has better ...

name: Wine Focusing on WineD3D Vulkan Back-end (vs DXVK)
snippet: It looks like the Wine project are proceeding forward with their own WineD3D Vulkan back-end, rather than using the competing DXVK as seen in Valve's Proton ...

name: Wine DXVK - The Future with Vulkan | Page 2 - GamersOnLinux
snippet: You can also install DXVK for per application. Just place the correct dll files into the folder where the game exe is and set them to native in winecfg.
